title: RabbitMQ File Descriptor Utilization
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="rabbitmq:node"
| eval fd_pct=if(fd_total>0, round(fd_used/fd_total*100,1), null())
| where fd_pct > 75
| table _time, name, fd_used, fd_total, fd_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: High file-descriptor usage predicts `emfile` errors, connection drops, and cluster instability during connection storms.
value: Gives capacity teams time to raise `ulimit`, prune stale connections, or scale out before the broker refuses sockets.
grandmaExplanation: We use this to gives capacity teams time to raise, prune stale connections, or scale out before the broker refuses sockets.
implementation: Poll `/api/nodes` every minute. Confirm `fd_total` reflects the effective OS limit in your environment. Alert at 75% sustained; emergency page at 90%. Pair with connection churn dashboards.
visualization: Gauge (fd_pct), Line chart (fd_used trend), Table (nodes over threshold).
knownFalsePositives: Queues and broker metrics swing during rebalancing, replay, or maintenance. We align with change windows.
